root_cause.text: The `buck parser-cache` command uses the default Java `ObjectInputStream` to deserialize state data without restricting the types of classes that can be instantiated, leading to arbitrary code execution if a maliciously crafted object is provided.
root_cause.evidence: The patch replaces `ObjectInputStream` with a custom `ParserStateObjectInputStream` in `ParserCacheCommand.java`, which overrides the `resolveClass` method to enforce a strict whitelist that only allows deserialization of the `RemoteDaemonicParserState` class.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `buck parser-cache` command uses the default Java `ObjectInputStream` to deserialize state data without restricting the types of classes that can be instantiated, leading to arbitrary code execution if a maliciously crafted object is provided.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch replaces `ObjectInputStream` with a custom `ParserStateObjectInputStream` in `ParserCacheCommand.java`, which overrides the `resolveClass` method to enforce a strict whitelist that only allows deserialization of the `RemoteDaemonicParserState` class.

intent_record.what: Added an import statement for ParserStateObjectInputStream.
intent_record.how: Makes the ParserStateObjectInputStream class available in the current file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace ObjectInputStream with a specialized ParserStateObjectInputStream to control object deserialization.
intent_record.what: Changed the instantiation of ObjectInputStream to ParserStateObjectInputStream.
intent_record.how: Modifies the deserialization process to use a custom input stream, which likely restricts or customizes class resolution during object deserialization.

intent_record.intent_summary: Include ParserStateObjectInputStream.java in the library build configuration.
intent_record.what: Added ParserStateObjectInputStream.java to the source file list of a java_immutables_library build rule.
intent_record.how: Ensures the build system compiles and includes this new Java file in the resulting library.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a restricted ObjectInputStream to guard against unsafe Java deserialization.
intent_record.what: Added a new ParserStateObjectInputStream class that extends ObjectInputStream and implements a whitelist for class resolution.
intent_record.how: Restricts deserialization to only allow RemoteDaemonicParserState, preventing the instantiation of arbitrary classes and mitigating deserialization vulnerabilities.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports and ExpectedException rule to support new test cases.
intent_record.what: Added imports for Java I/O, collections, zip utilities, and JUnit ExpectedException, along with a new ExpectedException rule instance.
intent_record.how: Provides the necessary classes and test rules to perform file, stream, and zip operations, and to assert that specific exceptions are thrown during test execution.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a test case to verify that loading invalid parser cache data correctly throws an InvalidClassException.
intent_record.what: Added a new test method `testInvalidData` that writes invalid serialized data to a temporary zip file and attempts to load it.
intent_record.how: It verifies that the system properly detects invalid parser cache data and throws an expected `InvalidClassException` when a load operation is attempted.

grouping_result.intent: Add a restricted ObjectInputStream to guard against unsafe Java deserialization.
Include ParserStateObjectInputStream.java in the library build configuration.
Add imports and ExpectedException rule to support new test cases.
Add a test case to verify that loading invalid parser cache data correctly throws an InvalidClassException.

consistency_review.core_intent: Restrict ObjectInputStream usage to guard against unsafe Java deserialization

relevance_review.reason: The group adds an import for ParserStateObjectInputStream, which is a custom ObjectInputStream likely introduced to safely deserialize state data and prevent arbitrary code execution, directly addressing the vulnerability root cause.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by replacing the unsafe ObjectInputStream with a specialized ParserStateObjectInputStream that restricts the types of classes that can be deserialized.
The inclusion of ParserStateObjectInputStream.java directly relates to replacing the vulnerable default ObjectInputStream with a restricted custom implementation to prevent arbitrary code execution.
The group only adds imports and test rules (ExpectedException) for test setup, which does not directly address the insecure deserialization root cause.
The test case explicitly verifies the fix for the insecure deserialization vulnerability by ensuring that an InvalidClassException is thrown when invalid parser cache data is loaded.

candidates.rationale: This line instantiates the default ObjectInputStream which is unsafe for deserializing untrusted data, leading to arbitrary code execution.
This line performs the actual unsafe deserialization by calling readObject() on the default ObjectInputStream.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement instantiates the default ObjectInputStream which is unsafe for deserializing untrusted data, as it allows arbitrary classes to be instantiated.
This statement performs the actual unsafe deserialization by calling readObject() on the unrestricted ObjectInputStream, leading to arbitrary code execution.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Introducing buck parser-cache command

Summary:
Save a parser state to disk:
```
$ buck parser-cache --save [filename]
`
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit e6d6dfacb93311425f4c1336a1107c657e647bb9
Author: Hoa Dinh <dvh@fb.com>
Date:   Wed Dec 20 15:30:51 2017 -0800

    Introducing buck parser-cache command
    
    Summary:
    Save a parser state to disk:
    ```
    $ buck parser-cache --save [filename]
    ```
    
    Load a parser state from disk:
    ```
    $ buck parser-cache --load [filename]
    ```
    
    Test Plan: CI
    
    Reviewed By: ttsugriy
    
    fbshipit-source-id: f6e1638

diff --git a/src/com/facebook/buck/cli/Pa
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces `ParserCacheCommand.java` which uses the default `ObjectInputStream` to deserialize the parser state (`ois.readObject()`) from an arbitrary file path provided by the user. There is no type filtering mechanism in place to restrict the classes that can be instantiated during deserialization, which exposes the system to arbitrary code execution.
```
